This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor) >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
James J. John papers
John, James, 1928-
14-17-4295
7.2 cubic feet. 7.2 cubic feet.
1930-2016 1970-2010 Research material, photographs, publications, audio recordings Materials relating to the career of Professor James John. Items relate to medieval studies including intellectual history and universities, theology, Latin paleography, historiography, Preand#x301;montreand#x301;. Course materials including, lectures, notes, syllabi, exams, and annotated publications for his courses in the Department of History including courses on the Middle Ages, medieval culture 400-1300 and paleography. Files relating to History 263, 264, 365, 366, 501, and 665. Publications owned by or relating to Harry Caplan, Dorothy Schullian, John J. Waldemer. Many slides and images of medieval and other manuscripts some of which have been digitized. Materials from and relating to E. A. (Elias Avery) Lowe including a set of glass lantern slides of medieval manuscripts belonging to Lowe, correspondence, publications, transcriptions of manuscripts, biographical material. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 8 ======= 8 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 10316373
Slide images
Photographs
Photographs
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Katie Guttman
Date completed:
March 2018
EAD encoding:
Fredrika Loew, March 2018
Date modified:
Marcie Farwell, May 2018
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
James John is a Cornell University professor emeritus, where he taught medieval intellectual history; historiography; universities; Latin paleography in the Department of History.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Materials relating to the career of Professor James John. Items relate to medieval studies including intellectual history and universities, theology, Latin paleography, historiography, Preand#x301;montreand#x301;. Course materials including, lectures, notes, syllabi, exams, and annotated publications for his courses in the Department of History including courses on the Middle Ages, medieval culture 400-1300 and paleography. Files relating to History 263, 264, 365, 366, 501, and 665. Publications owned by or relating to Harry Caplan, Dorothy Schullian, John J. Waldemer. Many slides and images of medieval and other manuscripts some of which have been digitized. Materials from and relating to E. A. (Elias Avery) Lowe including a set of glass lantern slides of medieval manuscripts belonging to Lowe, correspondence, publications, transcriptions of manuscripts, biographical material.
RELATED MATERIALS
Additional Dorothy Schullian material located in collection #4131, Dorothy May Schullian papers.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Coursework
Series II. E.A. Lowe files
Series III. Slides, films, and microfilms
Series IV. General documents
Cite As:
James J. John papers, #14-17-4295. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Paleography, Latin. Paleography. Middle Ages -- Study and teaching. Medievalism -- Study and teaching. Historiography. Caplan, Harry, 1896-1980. Lowe, E. A. (Elias Avery), 1879-1969. Schullian, Dorothy M. (Dorothy May), 1906-1989.
